mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within a single month signals deteriorating labor demand, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 48% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and industrial production indicates synchronized real-economy expansion, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window, or periods of simultaneous HOUST and INDPRO declines do not produce significantly lower forward returns than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active rate hikes, rising short-term policy rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero during consecutive 3-month FEDFUNDS increases, or the hypothesis yields a negative Sharpe ratio over any 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising personal financial assets support consumer spending capacity, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-month correlation between RSAFS changes and SPX 5-day returns falls below 0.10, or the signal produces a negative information coefficient across all market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When total nonfarm payrolls exceed 150,000 for three consecutive months, it confirms robust labor market health, reducing recession fears and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns average below 0% during the triggered periods, or the signal's hit rate drops below 50% over a 4-year walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in personal financial assets and rising unemployment indicate a synchronized demand shock that erodes corporate cash flow expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve predictive power over single-series models (AIC difference < 2), or forward returns during joint deterioration periods are not statistically lower than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals sustained manufacturing output, which supports corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit a positive skew during the specified regime, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.
